chapter_title.en: Chapter 55 - The Fasting of the menstruating and the post-menstruation bleeding woman

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/339)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/339)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) الْحَائِضُ تَقْضِي الصَّوْمَ قَالَ نَعَمْ قُلْتُ تَقْضِي الصَّلَاةَ قَالَ لَا قُلْتُ مِنْ أَيْنَ جَاءَ هَذَا قَالَ أَوَّلُ مَنْ قَاسَ إِبْلِيسُ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Al Hassan Bin Rashid who said,

‘I said to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, ‘The menstruating woman would make up for the (missed) Fast?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Yes’. I said, ‘She would make up for the (missed) Prayer?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: No. ‘I said, ‘Where does this come from?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The first one to analogise was Iblees<sup>la</sup>’.
en.sarwar: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from al-Hassan ibn Rashid who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, if a woman experiencing Hayd (menses) is required to complete Qada’ (compensatory fast) of the fast. He (the Imam) said, ‘Yes, she is required to complete its Qada’ (compensatory fast).’ I then asked if she is required to perform Qada’ (compensatory salat) of Salat (prayer)?’ He (the Imam) said, ‘No, she is not required to perform Qada’ (compensatory salat) for Salat (prayer).’ I then asked, ‘On what basis is it?’ He (the Imam) said, ‘The first one who analogized was Iblis (Satan).”’
